title: The Primitives
scan: What you will learn: The five primitives every component is assembled from вҖ” Events/Triggers, Functions/Operators, Variables, Storage, and Collection Heads вҖ” and why naming them precisely makes code review actionable

Why it matters: An architect who can name what is wrong at the primitive level gives engineers a fix target, not a feeling; "this function closes over the wrong variable" is reviewable, "this feels off" is not

The three storage shapes вҖ” simple, grouped, attached вҖ” have distinct browser-lifetime equivalents (memory, session, persistent) with different security and compliance profiles; the wrong shape is a GDPR risk, not just technical debt
Collection Heads are the most underused primitive вҖ” teams that skip them write fetch logic inside render functions and cannot cache, paginate, or test data access in isolation

blocks.html: he irreducible parts. Everything in Part I is built from these five, and an architect's framework is just these five seen from orbit.

cards.title: Events / Triggers
cards.body: The moments that start work вҖ” a click, a timer, an inbound request, a state change.

cards.title: Functions / Operators
cards.body: The verbs. Actions and transformations that turn inputs into outputs.

cards.title: Variables
cards.body: Named storage for a single value. The smallest unit of memory you control.

cards.title: Storage
cards.body: Simple values, grouped collections (lists, maps), or attached references between them.

cards.title: Heads of Collections
cards.body: The handle into a group вҖ” a registry, root or manager you ask instead of touching items directly.

items: Simple вҖ” a lone value: a name, a count, a flag.
Grouped вҖ” many of a kind kept together: an array, an object, a map.
Attached вҖ” a value that points at another structure: a relationship or reference.

blocks.html: Helper functions exist to build and maintain these groups вҖ” and the "head" (a manager or registry) is what the rest of the system talks to, so no one reaches into the raw collection by hand.

sections.title: Storage lifetimes вҖ” the compliance boundary

blocks.html: Storage shape is a compliance decision. PII stored in localStorage persists across sessions and is accessible to any script on the same origin вҖ” including injected third-party analytics tags. The difference between localStorage and an HttpOnly cookie is the difference between GDPR compliance and a GDPR breach.

Storing derived state in storage instead of recomputing on read. A "formatted price" stored in localStorage becomes stale the moment the currency or locale changes. Store source data and the parameters needed to derive from it; compute derived values on read.

blocks.label: Architect's Review В· Primitives
items: Where does each primitive live in the codebase?
Are storage shapes (simple / grouped / attached) used consistently?
Are heads of collections used, or do views reach into raw data?
Are helper functions over-coupled to specific groups?
